entities.name: BRASSICA JUNCEA
entities.description: The plant species Brassica juncea cv. RIK-81-1 used for shoot and root differentiation studies

entities.name: SHOOT REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of inducing the formation of shoots from excised cotyledon explants

entities.name: COTYLEDONS
entities.description: The embryonic leaves used as explants for organogenesis

entities.name: MURASHIGE AND SKOOG MEDIUM
entities.description: The basal nutrient medium used for culturing explants

entities.name: N6-BENZYLADENINE
entities.description: A cytokinin used to induce shoot bud differentiation

entities.name: SHOOT BUDS
entities.description: The adventitious structures formed during shoot regeneration

entities.name: ROOTS
entities.description: The structures formed when cotyledons are cultured on MS medium alone

entities.name: ORGANOGENESIS
entities.description: The process of organ formation from the petiole of the cotyledon

entities.name: PETIOLE
entities.description: The stalk of the cotyledon where shoot or root formation was restricted

entities.name: LAMINA
entities.description: The blade of the cotyledon which did not exhibit organogenesis

entities.name: GENETIC TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: The process of introducing foreign DNA into the plant, for which the cotyledon system is potentially useful

entities.name: MORPHOGENESIS
entities.description: The biological process that can be studied using the B. juncea cotyledon system
